expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report has an Options Analysis section that is more than 5 lines
expectations.evidence: Options Analysis section spans lines 112-183 — approximately 70 lines of detailed IV analysis, flow analysis, 3 strategy alternatives, timing considerations, and verdict

expectations.text: Report mentions IV Rank or IV Percentile in the options analysis
expectations.evidence: Line 125: 'IV Rank (est.): ~55-65%' and line 126: 'IV Percentile (est.): ~60-70%' in a structured table

expectations.text: Report specifically evaluates the put-selling strategy the user asked about
expectations.evidence: Lines 144-170: Three named strategies evaluated — CSP at $17 (recommended), put credit spread $16/$14, and aggressive ATM $18. Each with strike, premium, breakeven, and risk analysis.

expectations.text: Report recommends for or against selling puts with reasoning tied to IV environment
expectations.evidence: Line 181: 'YES — selling puts on SOFI is well-supported by the data. The IV > HV setup, combined with a stock that\'s been hammered 42% despite record fundamentals and insider buying, creates an attractive premium-harvesting opportunity.'

expectations.text: Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit action (BUY/SELL/HOLD or options strategy)
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section with multiples
expectations.evidence: Lines 57-68: Valuation Summary with P/E (FWD) 23.8x, EV/EBITDA ~17-19x, P/S 5.0x, P/B 2.3x with historical averages and peer medians

expectations.text: Report has fundamental analysis providing context for the options play
expectations.evidence: Lines 187-231: Full Fundamental Analysis with Q4 2025 records, FY2025 results, 2026 guidance, competitive position, and management assessment

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
expectations.evidence: Lines 277-300: 22 sources with hyperlinks including AlphaQuery, CNBC, BusinessWire, Nasdaq options data

expectations.text: Report contains actual current SOFI price data (not fabricated)
expectations.evidence: Line 3: '$18.81 (-0.58% pre-market)' — consistent with SOFI trading data on March 10, 2026

claims.evidence: Sourced from AlphaQuery link provided in sources
Sourced from MarketBeat unusual options alert link
Corroborated across multiple sources (Yahoo Finance, FUNanc1al) and matches old_skill output

suggestions.reason: Both outputs pass all 11 assertions. The assertions don't discriminate between a basic options section and the deep multi-strategy analysis the with_skill produced. Consider assertions like 'Report provides at least 2 alternative options strategies with different risk profiles' or 'Report discusses timing considerations relative to upcoming earnings.'
No assertion checks whether IV is sourced from actual data (e.g., AlphaQuery) vs estimated. The with_skill version cites specific 30-Day IV Mean from AlphaQuery while the old_skill uses ranges.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. Both versions produced genuinely excellent options analysis — the old skill performed surprisingly well on this options-focused prompt. The with_skill version has more precise IV data (sourced vs estimated), more structured strategy alternatives, and timing considerations for earnings proximity, but the old_skill has a valuable Risk Summary and put-selling checklist.
